title: Submit a feedback or grievance entry
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to submit a feedback or grievance-related entry, so that my concern is formally captured and routed for role-based review.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am an authenticated Employee (User), when I open the Feedback and Grievance module, then I can create a new feedback or grievance-related entry.; Given I am creating an entry, when I fill in the entry, then I can capture the required details of my feedback or grievance concern.; Given I complete the required details, when I submit the entry, then the entry is recorded in the system and enters the role-based review workflow.; Given required details are missing, when I attempt to submit, then submission is blocked and I am prompted to complete the mandatory fields.; Given I have submitted an entry, when I revisit the module, then I can see the entry I raised along with its current status.
source: FR 6.19.1 (submission of feedback and grievance-related entries with role-based review)

title: Role-based review of submitted entries
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want submitted feedback and grievance entries routed to me for review based on my role, so that the right authorized reviewer handles each entry.
acceptance_criteria: Given an entry has been submitted, when I access the module in a review role, then I can view the entries assigned for my review.; Given I hold a reviewer role, when I open an entry, then I can review its details as part of the role-based review workflow.; Given I hold a reviewer role, when I act on an entry, then only the review actions permitted for my role are available to me.; Given a user does not hold a review role, when they attempt to review an entry, then the review actions are not available to them.; Given multiple reviewers exist, when entries are routed, then each entry is directed to reviewers authorized for that entry's scope.

title: Restrict access to grievance information
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want access to grievance-related information restricted to authorized personnel, so that sensitive concerns remain confidential.
acceptance_criteria: Given a user is not authorized, when they attempt to view grievance-related information, then access is denied.; Given a user is authorized, when they access grievance-related information, then only the entries within their authorization scope are visible.; Given grievance data exists, when access is granted, then it is limited strictly to authorized personnel.; Given an unauthorized user attempts access, when the attempt occurs, then it is prevented and, where applicable, logged for auditability.

title: Track the status of an entry
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to track the status of my submitted feedback or grievance entry, so that I know how it is progressing.
acceptance_criteria: Given I have submitted an entry, when I view it, then its current status is displayed.; Given the status of an entry changes, when I revisit the entry, then the updated status is reflected.; Given I have multiple entries, when I open the module, then I can see the status of each of my entries.

title: Update status during review
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to update the status of a feedback or grievance entry as I review it, so that its progress is accurately reflected.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am an authorized reviewer, when I review an entry, then I can update its status.; Given I update an entry's status, when the change is saved, then the new status is recorded and visible to the relevant parties, including the submitter.; Given I am not an authorized reviewer, when I attempt to change an entry's status, then the action is not permitted.; Given a status change occurs, when it is saved, then it is captured in the entry's audit trail.

title: Maintain an audit trail
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the module to maintain an audit trail of feedback and grievance activity, so that actions on each entry are traceable and accountable.
acceptance_criteria: Given an entry is submitted, reviewed, or updated, when the action occurs, then it is recorded for auditability.; Given an action is recorded, when it is captured, then the audit record identifies the action taken and the associated context needed for accountability.; Given an audit trail exists, when an authorized user inspects an entry, then the recorded history of actions on that entry is available.; Given a user is not authorized, when they attempt to view the audit trail, then access is denied.

title: Scope module to Phase 1 needs
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want the module scoped to Phase 1 feedback and grievance tracking needs, so that current requirements are met while broader case-management is deferred to a future phase.
acceptance_criteria: Given the Phase 1 release, when the module is used, then it supports feedback and grievance tracking without full case-management workflows.; Given broader case-management capabilities are planned for a future phase, when Phase 1 scope is defined, then only feedback and grievance tracking features are included.; Given the module is delivered, when Phase 1 capabilities are enabled, then submission, role-based review, access restriction, status tracking, and auditability are supported as the in-scope feature set.
source: FR 6.19.4 (Phase 1 scope; case-management deferred to future phase)

title: Submit an entry on behalf of a non-user employee
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to submit a feedback or grievance-related entry on behalf of an Employee (Non-User), so that employees without system access can still have their concerns formally captured and reviewed.
acceptance_criteria: Given an Employee (Non-User) has no system access, when they raise a concern to me, then I can create a feedback or grievance-related entry on their behalf.; Given I create an entry on behalf of a non-user employee, when I record it, then the entry captures the concerned employee's identity and required details.; Given the entry is submitted, when it is recorded, then it enters the same role-based review workflow as directly submitted entries; Given the entry exists, when it is inspected, then the audit trail reflects that it was submitted on behalf of the non-user employee.
source: FR 6.19.1 (submission of feedback and grievance-related entries; Employee (Non-User) coverage)

title: Scoped review and oversight across group companies
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want role-based review and access to feedback and grievance entries limited to the companies within my group, so that oversight respects organizational and confidentiality boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: Given entries exist across multiple companies, when I access the module, then I can view and review only the entries belonging to companies within my group scope.; Given an entry is outside my group scope, when I attempt to access it, then access is denied.; Given I hold a reviewer role, when I review entries within my scope, then role-based review and status update actions are available to me for those entries.; Given I access grievance information, when it is displayed, then it remains restricted to authorized personnel within my authorization scope.
source: FR 6.19.1, FR 6.19.2 (role-based review; access restricted to authorized personnel)

title: Portfolio-level access and configuration
user_story: As a Portfolio Admin, I want authorized, scope-limited access to feedback and grievance tracking across my portfolio, so that I can oversee the process and ensure the module is available and correctly restricted for each company.
acceptance_criteria: Given the module is provisioned, when I configure it for companies in my portfolio, then feedback and grievance tracking, role-based review, and access restrictions are enabled per company.; Given entries exist across my portfolio, when I access grievance-related information, then only entries within my authorized portfolio scope are visible.; Given a user is unauthorized, when access controls are applied, then grievance information remains restricted to authorized personnel.; Given oversight is required, when I inspect activity, then auditability and status tracking are available within my authorization scope.
source: FR 6.19.1, FR 6.19.2, FR 6.19.3 (role-based review; access restriction; auditability and status tracking)

title: Confidential handling of my grievance
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want my grievance-related information handled confidentially and shared only with authorized reviewers, so that I can raise sensitive concerns without exposure.
acceptance_criteria: Given I submit a grievance-related entry, when it is stored, then its information is restricted to authorized personnel only.; Given other employees do not have authorization, when they attempt to view my entry, then access is denied.; Given I view my own entry, when I open it, then I can see my submission and its current status.; Given confidentiality applies, when my entry is reviewed, then only authorized reviewers within scope can access its details.
source: FR 6.19.2 (access restricted to authorized personnel); FR 6.19.3 (status tracking)
